sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Daedalus Wallet Maintenance and Improvements 2026-2027 delivered by Se7en Labs, Inc. as a time and materials engagement covering protocol maintenance, ecosystem expansion, user support, and oversight administration.
Recipient: Funds are to be held and administered by Intersect with monthly disbursement to Se7en Labs against verified work.

Deliverables: Node and wallet backend updates, hard fork readiness, platform support, release engineering, and signed releases for 52 weeks, Compatible Daedalus stable release at least 2 weeks before each mainnet hard fork, Leios and Peras compatible node readiness ahead of testnet or mainnet activation, Keystone and Flex hardware wallet support in a stable release, CIP-30 dApp connector implementation with documentation and interoperability verification, Ongoing English and Japanese user support with full Japanese translation across new features, Public architecture assessment by Q3 2027, Independent financial audit and Intersect milestone-based administration
Deadline/expiry: Contract year 2026-2027; architecture assessment by Q3 2027; IOG contract described as closing August 31, 2026.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Daedalus is presented as Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet that runs an embedded Cardano node and derives wallet and governance data directly from chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Daedalus private keys are generated and stored on the user's own device and never transmitted to an external service, with open-source Apache 2.0 code allowing auditability.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Opt-in telemetry indicates approximately 4,000 monthly active Daedalus users, while the proposer asserts the true count is meaningfully higher because privacy-conscious users are less likely to opt in.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The team says it assumed Daedalus responsibility under an IOG contract in January 2026 and shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD/LSM Backend integration, Apple Silicon builds, the drt release CLI, Nix build modernization, and Daedalus 8.0 and 11.0.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal commits to compatible Daedalus releases at least 2 weeks before every mainnet hard fork and says release timing and version-currency metrics are independently verifiable from public GitHub repositories and the Cardano mainnet chain.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer states all funded outputs, including source code, build tooling, documentation, and release artifacts, will remain public assets under the Apache 2.0 license and that Se7en Labs does not monetize Daedalus usage.
missing: Independent evidence for: Daedalus is presented as Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet that runs an embedded Cardano node and derives wallet and governance data directly from chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends.

Independent evidence for: Opt-in telemetry indicates approximately 4,000 monthly active Daedalus users, while the proposer asserts the true count is meaningfully higher because privacy-conscious users are less likely to opt in.

Independent evidence for: The team says it assumed Daedalus responsibility under an IOG contract in January 2026 and shipped Mithril Snapshot Bootstrap, UTxO-HD/LSM Backend integration, Apple Silicon builds, the drt release CLI, Nix build modernization, and Daedalus 8.0 and 11.0.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states all funded outputs, including source code, build tooling, documentation, and release artifacts, will remain public assets under the Apache 2.0 license and that Se7en Labs does not monetize Daedalus usage.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Daedalus private keys are generated and stored on the user's own device and never transmitted to an external service, with open-source Apache 2.0 code allowing auditability." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Daedalus is presented as Cardano's only full-node desktop wallet that runs an embedded Cardano node and derives wallet and governance data directly from chain without third-party APIs or trusted backends." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
